title: Phegopteris incisa
intro: Phegopteris incisa, also known as the cut-leaf fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, characterized by its delicate, lacy fronds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and improve overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Phegopteris incisa include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phegopteris incisa is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Phegopteris incisa is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This fern is native to various regions, including Asia and the Pacific Islands. It is a member of the genus Phegopteris, which includes several other species of ferns. Phegopteris incisa is further classified into the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. It is a part of the kingdom Plantae, consisting of various types of plants, including ferns and conifers.
